title: 「和理非出動」一齊寫信向Starbucks總公司投訴

replies.msg:

Template: （英文廢j, 高手可以幫手改吓）

Dear Sir/Madam,

I would like to suggest your team to re-consider the franchising relationship with Maxim's Caterers Limited (Maxim) in Hong Kong.

I was a loyal customer of Starbucks for more than 5 years, drinking Caffe Latte or Cappuccino everyday. However, Hong Kong Starbucks would no longer be my option.

In recent months, HongKongers are protesting freedom and democracy against Hong Kong Government. However, Annie Wu Suk Ching, the daughter of Maxim founder James Tak Wu, called Hong Kong protesters "rioters", and said we do not represent Hong Kong, and she publicly criticized us without reasonable reasons. Everthough Maxim declared that Annie had no any official relationship with the company, Maxim will still be the target of protests.

Please kindly re-consider the franchising relationship with Maxim to maintain your business reputation in Hong Kong.

Yours sincerely,
A HongKonger


麻煩大家幫幫手推
要令美心冇咗Starbucks同元氣
除咗裝修都要執筆
屌 係喎

其實段嘢好差
有冇英文高手幫手執執佢

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to urge your company to consider terminating the franchise to Maxim's Caterers Limited (Hong Kong) immediately because of the pro-Beijing comments made by Annie Wu Suk Ching, the daughter of Maxim's founder James Tak Wu.

In recent months, HongKongers have been fighting for freedom and democracy against Hong Kong Government. On Sep 11, however, Ms. Wu, appeared at the United Nations Human Rights Council representing the Hong Kong Federation of Women, a group with close ties to the Chinese Communist Party. She called the Hong Kong protesters as "small group of radical protesters" and that they do not represent all Hongkongers. Even though Maxim's has distanced itself from Wu by saying that she does not hold any position at the company, Maxim's is, and will be a target of the boycott campaign.

I hereby urge your company to terminate the franchise to Maxim's as it does not represent the social and corporate values of your company.

Yours faithfully,
A Hong Kong citizen

replies.msg:
唔該晒你發起運動。我幫你改咗少少英文，俾你參考吓。

Dear Sir/Madam,

I would like to suggest that your company end the franchise relationship with Maxim's Caterers Limited (Maxim’s) in Hong Kong.

I have been a loyal customer of Starbucks for more than 5 years, drinking Caffe Latte or Cappuccino every day. However, Hong Kong Starbucks would no longer be my option. I am going to explain why.

In recent months, HongKongers have been fighting for freedom and democracy against the tyranny of the Hong Kong Government by peacefully protests. However, the Government repeatedly denied our rights to free speech, and allowed the Hong Kong police to indiscriminately attack and arrest a huge number of citizens, causing large scale casualties and widespread terror in the city. Though two million Hongkongers took to the streets in June against the anti-extradition law, Annie Wu Suk Ching, the daughter of Maxim’s founder James Tak Wu, called Hong Kong protesters "rioters", and said they do not represent the voice of Hong Kong people.

Also, she gave a misleading speech about Hong Kong protests at the United Nations Human Rights Council in September this year by beautifying the brutal acts of the Government and claiming that the protestors were hijacked. Though Maxim declared that Annie did not have any official relationship with the company, a large number of Hong Kong people were furious with her irresponsible speech and started a boycott against Starbucks.

Starbucks will continue to be the target of protests if the company does not fulfill its corporate responsibility by defending the core values of Hong Kong, namely, democracy and freedom. As a long time fan, I will be extremely sad to see Starbucks being targeted by disappointed citizens and being forced to close.

Please kindly consider ending the franchise relationship with Maxim’s to maintain your business reputation in Hong Kong.

Yours sincerely,
A HongKonger

Starbucks回覆

Thank you for contacting Starbucks. I just finished reading your email and appreciate you taking the time to share your concerns with us.

Travis, I am sorry to hear that about the inconvenience for the comments made by this person, I absolutely understand your concerns. I will be more than glad to assist you.

Please know that receiving your feedback is very important and valuable to us, because it helps to determine which areas we are doing well in, and those we can improve.

Rest assured that I've shared your comments with all of the appropriate international departments for their review. We appreciate you brought this to our attention.

Thanks again for writing us. If you ever have any questions or concerns in the future, please don't hesitate to get in touch!

Have a wonderful day!

Sincerely,
Ronaldo M.
Starbucks Customer Care
